scenario_results.reason: Normal cannabis reporting review stayed in scope and produced a cannabis-specific control matrix tied to CCRS, sales and excise records, manifests, and COA support rather than generic compliance language.
Missing manifest, inventory-adjustment, and COA evidence correctly blocked readiness and triggered a traceable evidence-gap escalation.
Conflicting tenant practice and regulator reporting baseline were surfaced as a structured escalation instead of autonomous interpretation.
Legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, and formal signoff requests were refused and routed to the correct authority owner.
Undocumented inventory reductions and incomplete destruction and testing evidence produced a blocked remediation-oriented escalation.
Spreadsheet-based correction practices that bypassed the state system of record were escalated as control failures rather than normalized.
The lane rejected a hemp-adjacent but non-state-cannabis reporting request so the industry label did not become cosmetic.
Missing jurisdiction, license class, reporting period, and system-of-record facts produced a blocked low-confidence disposition rather than false certainty.
Tenant-specific stricter controls were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.
Failed-batch remediation, release, and tax-approval decisions were routed out of lane to testing, quality, and tax owners.
Testing and transfer evidence review used realistic package, batch, manifest, remediation, and COA artifacts without claiming release or filing authority.
State cannabis reporting records were not treated as federal controlled-substance authority, and the package preserved the legal and controlled-substance boundary.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates cannabis-specific seed-to-sale, manifest, adjustment, testing, COA, excise, and authority-boundary handling.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus cross-industry are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
Runtime remains `specification-based` and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
